subject: [PATCH v2 10/13] nfsd: add netlink upcall for the svc_export cache

reviews.author: Jeff Layton
reviews.summary: Identified a leftover attribute from an earlier version that can be safely removed. Provided a fixed patch and suggested removing it to regenerate the headers.

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary: Acknowledged Jeff's catch and suggested waiting to see if there are other review comments.
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, Mar 30, 2026, at 10:49 AM, Jeff Layton wrote:
> On Mon, 2026-03-30 at 10:22 -0400, Chuck Lever wrote:
>> > 
>> > +  -
>> > +    name: svc-export-req
>> > +    attributes:
>> > +      -
>> > +        name: seqno
>> > +        type: u64
>> > +      -
>> > +        name: client
>> > +        type: string
>> > +      -
>> > +        name: path
>> > +        type: string
>> 
>> Is the svc-export-req attribute set used for anything?
>> 
>> 
>
> No, good catch.
>
> That's a holdover from an earlier version that had a separate attribute
> set for the up and downcalls. It's not needed in the latest version.
>
> It should be fine to remove it and regenerate the headers. The result
> builds fine without it. I've pushed a fixed patch to the 'exportd-nl'
> branch in my tree. You can fix it up or I can resend if you prefer.

Thanks for jumping on that. Let's wait to see if there are other review
comments.


-- 
Chuck Lever

2026-03-30.patch_summary: A minor issue was identified in the patch, specifically a leftover attribute from an earlier version that is no longer needed.
